---
title: "Orders"
description: "Create and manage orders via the Vendor API."
---

## Authentication

All Vendor API endpoints require an `X-API-Key` header with a vendor-scoped key.
Keys begin with `ubx_test_` (test mode) or `ubx_live_` (production).

```http
X-API-Key: ubx_test_your_vendor_key
```

## List orders

`GET /api/vendor/v1/orders`

Returns a paginated list of orders for the authenticated vendor. Supports
filtering by status and date range.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | string | Filter by order status (`draft`, `confirmed`, `assigned`, `dispatched`, `delivered`, `cancelled`) |
| `page` | integer | Page number (default: 1) |
| `page_size` | integer | Results per page (default: 25, max: 100) |

## Create order

`POST /api/vendor/v1/orders`

Creates a new delivery order. The endpoint is idempotent on `external_reference`
— if an order with the same reference already exists it is returned with HTTP 200
instead of creating a duplicate.

```json
{
  "external_reference": "SHOP-ORDER-1001",
  "client_name": "Jane Mwangi",
  "client_phone": "+254712345678",
  "description": "2x T-shirts, size L",
  "quantity": 2,
  "unit_price_kes": 1500,
  "pickup_type": "delivery",
  "is_cod": false
}
```

### COD orders

Set `is_cod: true` and provide `cod_amount_kes` to mark the order as
cash-on-delivery. The rider dashboard will display a COD badge with the
collection amount.

## Bulk import via CSV

### Step 1 — Download the template

`GET /api/vendor/v1/orders/csv-template`

Returns `urbnbox-orders-template.csv` with the correct headers and two example
rows. No authentication required.

**Column order:**

| # | Column | Required | Notes |
|---|--------|----------|-------|
| 1 | `external_reference` | Yes | Your order ID — used for idempotency |
| 2 | `client_name` | Yes | Recipient full name |
| 3 | `client_phone` | Yes | Kenyan number (`07xx` or `+254xx`) |
| 4 | `description` | Yes | Item description |
| 5 | `quantity` | Yes | Positive integer |
| 6 | `unit_price_kes` | Yes | Price in KES, e.g. `1500` |
| 7 | `pickup_type` | Yes | `delivery` or `locker` |

Example:
```csv
external_reference,client_name,client_phone,description,quantity,unit_price_kes,pickup_type
ORD-001,Jane Doe,0712345678,Blue hoodie size M,2,1500,delivery
ORD-002,John Smith,0798765432,Running shoes size 42,1,4500,locker
```

### Step 2 — Upload for validation

`POST /api/vendor/v1/orders/import-csv`

Upload your filled CSV as `multipart/form-data` with field name `file`. The
response validates every row and returns per-row results — it does **not**
create orders automatically. Review the `rows` array, fix any errors, then
re-upload or switch to the JSON `/api/vendor/v1/orders/bulk` endpoint to commit.

```json
{
  "valid_count": 47,
  "error_count": 2,
  "total_count": 49,
  "rows": [
    {
      "row_num": 1,
      "external_reference": "ORD-001",
      "client_name": "Jane Doe",
      "client_phone": "0712345678",
      "description": "Blue hoodie size M",
      "quantity": 2,
      "unit_price_cents": 150000,
      "pickup_type": "delivery"
    },
    {
      "row_num": 12,
      "external_reference": "ORD-012",
      "client_name": "",
      "error": "client_name required"
    }
  ]
}
```
